name: Document
summary: This is the interface to a LayOut document. A Document is the 2D drawing that the user is working with, and it serves as the “entry point” for most Ruby API interactions. The Document.open method gives you a handle to a Document, and from there you can use the document-level methods to start getting information and making changes.

constructors.name: initialize
constructors.summary: The #initialize method creates a new Layout::Document. Passing a path to an existing Layout::Document will use that file as a template. The new Layout::Document won't have a path until it is saved for the first time.
constructors.remarks: The #initialize method creates a new Layout::Document. Passing a path to an existing Layout::Document will use that file as a template. The new Layout::Document won't have a path until it is saved for the first time.

params.name: template_path
params.doc: The path to the Layout::Document to use as a template

throws.when: if there was an error reading the template file
if the template file could not be found

methods.name: open
methods.summary: The open method creates a new Layout::Document by loading an existing .
methods.remarks: The open method creates a new Layout::Document by loading an existing .layout file.

params.name: path
params.doc: The path to the .layout file on disk.

returns.doc: The Layout::Document created from the .layout file.

throws.when: if the file does not exist

methods.name: add_entity
methods.signature: add_entity(entity, layer, page) => Layout::Entity | add_entity(entity, layer) => Layout::Entity
methods.summary: The #add_entity method adds an Entity to the Layout::Document and places it on the given Layer and Page.
methods.remarks: The #add_entity method adds an Entity to the Layout::Document and places it on the given Layer and Page. If layer is a shared Layer then page may be ommitted. The Entity must not already belong to a Layout::Document. If the Entity is a Group, then the Group along with all of its children will be added to the Layout::Document.

params.name: layer
params.doc: The Layer to add the Entity to

params.name: page
params.doc: The Page to add the Entity to

returns.doc: The Entity that was added to the Layout::Document.

throws.when: if no Page is passed in and layer is non-shared
if page does not belong to the Layout::Document
if layer does not belong to the Layout::Document
if entity already belongs to a Layout::Document

methods.name: attribute_dictionary
methods.summary: The #attribute_dictionary method returns a copy of the document's attribute dictionary with the given name.
methods.remarks: The #attribute_dictionary method returns a copy of the document's attribute dictionary with the given name. is no attribute dictionary

returns.doc: A copy of the document's attribute dictionary, or nil if there

methods.name: auto_text_definitions
methods.summary: The #auto_text_definitions method returns an array of AutoTextDefinition's in the Layout::Document.
methods.remarks: The #auto_text_definitions method returns an array of AutoTextDefinition's in the Layout::Document.

methods.name: delete_attribute
methods.summary: The #delete_attribute method is used to delete an attribute from a document.
methods.remarks: The #delete_attribute method is used to delete an attribute from a document.

params.name: dictionary_name
params.doc: The name of an attribute dictionary.

methods.name: export
methods.summary: The #export method exports the Layout::Document to a given file format.
methods.remarks: The #export method exports the Layout::Document to a given file format. It knows which format to export based on the file extension you place on the file name. For example, a filename of “thing.pdf” will export a PDF file, whereas “thing.png” will export a set of PNG images. For LayOut version 2020.1, valid extensions include .pdf, .jpg, and .png.

params.name: file_path
params.doc: The file or image set to create. The directory path must already exist. The path must include the file extension.

params.name: options
params.doc: An optional hash of settings for the export.

throws.when: if an options value is the wrong type
if an options value is out of range
if the full file path does not exist
if the specified file type is missing or not supported

methods.name: get_attribute
methods.summary: The #get_attribute method is used to retrieve the value of an attribute in the document's attribute dictionary.
methods.remarks: The #get_attribute method is used to retrieve the value of an attribute in the document's attribute dictionary. If the third parameter, default_value, is not passed and there is no attribute that matches the given name, it returns nil. If default_value is provided and there is no matching attribute it returns the given value. It does not create an attribute with that name though.

params.name: name
params.doc: The name of an attribute dictionary.

params.name: default_value
params.type: String, Boolean, Integer, Float, Hash, Layout::Dictionary, nil
params.doc: A default value to return if no attribute is found.

returns.type: String, Boolean, Integer, Float, Layout::Dictionary, nil

methods.name: grid
methods.summary: The #grid method returns the Grid for a Layout::Document.
methods.remarks: The #grid method returns the Grid for a Layout::Document.

methods.name: grid_snap_enabled?
methods.summary: The #grid_snap_enabled? method returns whether or not grid snap is enabled in the Layout::Document.
methods.remarks: The #grid_snap_enabled? method returns whether or not grid snap is enabled in the Layout::Document.

methods.name: layers
methods.summary: The #layers method returns the Layers of the Layout::Document.
methods.remarks: The #layers method returns the Layers of the Layout::Document.

methods.name: object_snap_enabled?
methods.summary: The #object_snap_enabled? method returns whether or not inference is enabled in the Layout::Document.
methods.remarks: The #object_snap_enabled? method returns whether or not inference is enabled in the Layout::Document.

methods.name: page_info
methods.summary: The #page_info method returns a reference to the PageInfo settings of the Layout::Document.
methods.remarks: The #page_info method returns a reference to the PageInfo settings of the Layout::Document.

methods.name: pages
methods.summary: The #pages method returns the Pages of the Layout::Document.
methods.remarks: The #pages method returns the Pages of the Layout::Document. @example:

methods.name: path
methods.summary: The #path method returns the full path of the Layout::Document file.
methods.remarks: The #path method returns the full path of the Layout::Document file. An empty string is returned for a new Layout::Document (one which has not been saved and opened).

methods.name: remove_entity
methods.summary: The #remove_entity method removes an Entity from the Layout::Document.
methods.remarks: The #remove_entity method removes an Entity from the Layout::Document. If entity is a Group, then the Group and all of its children will be removed from the Layout::Document.

throws.when: if entity does not belong to the Layout::Document

methods.name: save
methods.signature: save => Object | save(path, version = Layout::Document::VERSION_CURRENT) => Object
methods.summary: The #save method saves the Layout::Document to a file at the given path.
methods.remarks: The #save method saves the Layout::Document to a file at the given path. Passing an empty path string will save the Layout::Document at its current path.

params.name: path
params.doc: The path to the .layout file on disk.

throws.when: if the Layout::Document hasn't been saved with a path yet

methods.name: set_attribute
methods.summary: The #set_attribute method adds an attribute to the document's attribute dictionary.
methods.remarks: The #set_attribute method adds an attribute to the document's attribute dictionary.

params.name: name
params.doc: The name of an attribute dictionary. @param [String] key An attribute key. @param [String, Boolean, Integer, Float, Hash, Layout::Dictionary, nil] value The value for the attribute.

methods.name: shared_entities
methods.summary: The #shared_entities method returns the Entities that exist on shared Layers in the Layout::Document.
methods.remarks: The #shared_entities method returns the Entities that exist on shared Layers in the Layout::Document.

methods.name: time_created
methods.summary: The #time_created method returns the time when the Layout::Document was created.
methods.remarks: The #time_created method returns the time when the Layout::Document was created.

returns.doc: time when the Layout::Document was created

methods.name: time_modified
methods.summary: The #time_modified method returns the last time the Layout::Document was modified.
methods.remarks: The #time_modified method returns the last time the Layout::Document was modified.

returns.doc: time when the Layout::Document was last modified

methods.name: time_published
methods.summary: The #time_published method returns the time when the Layout::Document was published.
methods.remarks: The #time_published method returns the time when the Layout::Document was published.

returns.doc: time when the Layout::Document was published

properties.summary: The #== method checks to see if the two Layout::Documents are equal.
properties.remarks: The #== method checks to see if the two Layout::Documents are equal. This checks whether the Ruby Objects are pointing to the same internal object.

properties.name: grid_snap_enabled
properties.summary: The #grid_snap_enabled= method sets whether or not grid snap is enabled in the Layout::Document.
properties.remarks: The #grid_snap_enabled= method sets whether or not grid snap is enabled in the Layout::Document.

properties.name: object_snap_enabled
properties.summary: The #object_snap_enabled= method enables or disables inference in the Layout::Document.
properties.remarks: The #object_snap_enabled= method enables or disables inference in the Layout::Document.

properties.name: precision
properties.summary: The #precision method returns the precision for the Layout::Document.
properties.remarks: The #precision method returns the precision for the Layout::Document.

properties.name: render_mode_override
properties.summary: The #render_mode_override method returns the override setting for output render modes of SketchUpModels in the Layout::Document.
properties.remarks: The #render_mode_override method returns the override setting for output render modes of SketchUpModels in the Layout::Document.

properties.name: units
properties.summary: The #units method returns the units for the Layout::Document.
properties.remarks: The #units method returns the units for the Layout::Document.
